5. Conclusions
BDS-3 service signal PPP-B2b provides a promising opportunity for
realizing real-time PPP by broadcasting precise corrections for GNSS
users in China and surrounding areas, and the advantages of this
featured service show more significant in case of internet-based
communication is unavailable. In this paper, a real-time ocean PPP
was proposed based on BDS-3 P

T. Geng et al. M e a s u r e m e n t203(2022)111911
Fig. 12. RMS of pseudo-range multipath errors as a function of elevation angles for B1I and B3I signals of BDS-3 (left panel) and L1 and L2 signals of GPS (right
panel) in the ocean and land environments.
0.158 and 0.233 m in the two directions for GPS-only. The optimal
positioning accuracy is observed in GPS +BDS-3 with horizontal and
vertical accuracies are 0.046 and 0.069 m, respectively. GPS-only can
converge to better than 0.15 m in E and N components and 0.3 m in U
components after 56.4 min, while BDS-3-only needs 29.4 min. The
